channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: What you need to know about Adnan Syed's murder conviction
item.description: The legal battle over Adnan Syed’s murder conviction was scrutinized in the hit podcast “Serial” a decade ago.
item.dc:creator: Kimberlee Kruesi, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: In lawsuit filing, Pentagon says transgender troops can't serve unless they meet a warfighting need
item.description: The Pentagon has revealed the specifics of its new transgender troop policy in a court filing that says any service member or recruit who has been diagnosed with or treated for gender dysphoria is disqualified from serving — unless they can prove they meet a specific warfighting need and adhere to s
item.dc:creator: Tara Copp, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Hamas hands over bodies of 4 hostages to Israel as dozens of Palestinians leave Israeli prison
item.description: KHAN YOUNIS, Gaza Strip (AP) — Hamas handed over the bodies of four hostages to the Red Cross early Thursday in exchange for Israel's release of hundreds of Palestinian prisoners, days before the first phase of the ceasefire in the Gaza Strip was to end.
item.dc:creator: WAFAA SHURAFA, SAMY MAGDY and JOSEF FEDERMAN, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Trump administration says it's cutting 90% of USAID foreign aid contracts
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Trump administration said it is eliminating more than 90% of the U.S. Agency for International Development’s foreign aid contracts and $60 billion in overall U.S. assistance around the world, putting numbers on its plans to eliminate the majority of U.S.
item.dc:creator: ELLEN KNICKMEYER, MARK SHERMAN and MATTHEW LEE, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Taiwan condemns China for conducting shooting drills off its coast
item.description: TAIPEI, Taiwan (AP) — Taiwan on Thursday condemned China’s military exercises after Beijing designated an area to conduct shooting drills off the self-governed island’s southwest coast.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Bell tolls as New York marks the anniversary of the 1993 World Trade Center bombing
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — New York City on Wednesday marked the anniversary of the 1993 bombing at the old World Trade Center, when Islamic extremists first attempted to bring down the twin towers with 1,200 pounds of explosives in a parked rental van.
item.dc:creator: JOHN MINCHILLO, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Supreme Court hears arguments on straight woman's 'reverse discrimination' case
item.description: Marlean Ames filed suit against a state agency claiming she was not given a promotion because she is heterosexual.
item.dc:creator: Safia Samee Ali, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Hackers slam Michigan tribe for not negotiating after ransomware attack
item.description: A group called RansomHub has claimed responsibility for the cyberattack and submitted a letter to provide its “side of the events.
item.dc:creator: Matt Jaworowski, NewsNation

item.title: Judge weighs continued freedom for Adnan Syed in 'Serial' murder case
item.description: BALTIMORE (AP) — More than a decade after Adnan Syed’s murder case attracted massive interest through the hit podcast “Serial,” a Baltimore judge is considering a request to reduce his sentence to time served, which would allow him to remain free indefinitely even though his conviction still stands.
item.dc:creator: LEA SKENE and BRIAN WITTE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Tennessee rape suspect was released without bond late last year: Docs
item.description: Junior Amaya was also charged with aggravated burglary for breaking into the woman's home before he allegedly raped her.
item.dc:creator: Megan Fayard, NewsNation

item.title: Mystery illnesses claim over 50 lives in Congo: What we know
item.description: Unidentified illnesses in northwestern Congo have killed more than 50 people over the past five weeks, nearly half of them within hours after they felt sick.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Judge criticizes Justice Department's broad reading of Trump's Capitol riot pardons
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — A federal judge on Wednesday criticized the Justice Department's evolving position that a presidential pardon for a Kentucky man who stormed the Capitol also covers his conviction for illegally possessing guns at his home. U.S.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL KUNZELMAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Christianity in US stops decline after years of losses: Survey
item.description: Although down substantially from 2007, 44 percent of Americans said they pray at least once a day, a figure that has remained consistent in the last four years.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, KRON4

item.title: American Airlines flight discontinues landing to avoid departing plane in Virginia
item.description: The maneuver occurred less than two hours before another plane attempting to land at Chicago’s Midway Airport was forced to climb back into the sky to avoid another aircraft crossing the runway.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Agriculture secretary outlines plan to lower egg prices
item.description: “The Agriculture Department will invest up to $1 billion to curb this crisis and make eggs affordable again," Rollins wrote in an op-ed.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, KRON4

item.title: iPhone bug that could suggest replacing 'racist' with 'Trump' is getting fixed
item.description: Apple is fixing a bug within the dictation feature on some iPhones that briefly suggests the word “Trump” when a word with an R consonant is spoken, including "racist.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Trump shares AI video envisioning Gaza development with gold statue of himself
item.description: The video also features children holding gold balloons also made to look like Trump and a renovated beachfront with luxury hotels and money flying in the air.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: RFK Jr. targets childhood psychiatric drugs; doctors push back
item.description: Childhood psychiatrists insist the drugs, for attention-deficit/hyperactivity disorder (ADHD) and depression, are nonaddictive and proven safe and say they are more concerned about young Americans unable to access psychiatric medications that could help.
item.dc:creator: Joseph Choi, WGN 9

item.title: American Airlines jet pulls out of DC landing to avoid collision
item.description: An American Airlines jet avoided a collision with another plane while preparing to land at Reagan National Airport in Washington, D.C., on Tuesday morning.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: NTSB blames Southwest Chicago near miss on Flexjet crew 'failure' to listen to air traffic control
item.description: National Transportation Safety Board chair Jennifer Homendy says investigations are ongoing into the near-miss between a Southwest airliner and a Flexjet in Chicago.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: WATCH: Officers cut open man's 'narco wig' at Colombian airport to reveal cocaine
item.description: He may have been using his head, but a 40-year-old passenger wasn't able to get past Colombian airport security with a cocaine-stuffed "narco wig," police said Monday.
item.dc:creator: Jeremy Tanner, Fox 5

item.title: Things to know about the ruling blocking President Trump's refugee ban
item.description: SEATTLE (AP) — President Donald Trump's effort to suspend the system for resettling refugees in the U.S. is on hold after a federal judge in Seattle blocked it. U.S.
item.dc:creator: GENE JOHNSON, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Student loan borrowers face abrupt 180 as GOP budget plans threaten to raise payments
item.description: Student loan borrowers could see their payments increase under budget plans currently under discussion by House Republicans, a violent about-face after years of working with the Biden administration to lower payments and secure debt relief.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, WGN 9

item.title: US energy secretary touts nuclear power as tech sector's thirst for electricity grows
item.description: ALBUQUERQUE, N.M. (AP) — U.S. Energy Secretary Chris Wright says it’s critical that the nation be out in front when it comes to artificial intelligence, and that means having reliable and affordable sources of electricity to meet the growing demands of the technology sector.
item.dc:creator: SUSAN MONTOYA BRYAN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Woman injured on Harry Potter theme park ride awarded $7.25 million
item.description: A federal jury has awarded $7.25 million to a 74-year-old Arizona woman for a spine injury she suffered in a fall while exiting the Harry Potter and the Forbidden Journey ride at Universal Studios Hollywood.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: A midair collision in Arizona prompts questions about air traffic control towers
item.description: Tens of thousands of planes take off, land and perform touch-and-goes at the Marana Regional Airport in southern Arizona every year. Without an air traffic control tower, it's a calculated dance that requires communication by pilots.
item.dc:creator: SUSAN MONTOYA BRYAN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: WATCH: Man steals 2 bulldog puppies as another 'fakes seizure' to distract staff
item.description: Two bulldog puppies were taken from a pet store in Colorado after a man "allegedly faked a seizure to distract employees," the Arapahoe Sheriff's Office said.
item.dc:creator: Ashley Suter, WFLA, NBC

item.title: FCC chairman climbs broadcast tower to promote industry, jobs
item.description: The nation's top communications regulator made a stop in Baldwin County Monday morning and you might be surprised why.
item.dc:creator: Debbie Williams, WFLA, NBC

item.title: FCC chairman climbs Nexstar broadcast tower to promote industry, jobs
item.description: The nation's top communications regulator made a stop in Baldwin County Monday morning and you might be surprised why.
item.dc:creator: Debbie Williams, Fox 5

item.title: Why you won't receive a Supplemental Security Income check in March
item.description: It's the latest blip in the payment schedule but don't worry — beneficiaries will still receive all of their payments.
item.dc:creator: Addy Bink, ABC 4

item.title: What will win at a nailbiter Oscars? AP's film writers make their predictions
item.description: By the time the Oscars wind around after months of other award shows and constant handicapping, the prizes can feel almost predetermined. Not this year.
item.dc:creator: JAKE COYLE and LINDSEY BAHR, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Student loan borrowers face abrupt change as GOP budget plans threaten to raise payments
item.description: Student loan borrowers could see their payments increase under budget plans currently under discussion by House Republicans, a violent about-face after years of working with the Biden administration to lower payments and secure debt relief.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, Fox 5

item.title: Pope Francis is critical but stable with no new respiratory crises: Vatican
item.description: “In the morning, after receiving the Eucharist, he resumed work activities,” the Vatican statement said.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5
